action.hypothesis: `factorized_topk` with `EF=8` is a stronger cost-feasible probe than the rejected soft-codebook attempt because it is directly LUTurbo-compatible, keeps large `K=128`, and has substantial encoder-cost headroom (`~1.12x h*n`, well below the `1.5x` cap). In the new low-EF cost-constrained regime, its cheaper selector may deliver a better total cost/FVU tradeoff than more expressive but budget-riskier residual/codebook families.
action.summary: Probe `factorized_topk` at `EF=8` to establish a cost-feasible LUTurbo-compatible point with clear export semantics and meaningful margin under the `selection_cost <= 1.5x h*n` constraint. This tests whether a cheaper direct-compatible selector becomes preferable once the search is forced into the low-cost regime.
action.self_review: This is a clean single-hypothesis cost-exploration round. Risk: historical `factorized_topk` quality was weak at high EF, so low-EF feasibility may still come with poor FVU; however the current blocker is absence of any budget-feasible compatible point, so direct-compatible cost headroom is the priority.
notes_to_memory: Reconstruction formula: `x_hat = sum_i alpha_i b_i`. The factorized hidden layer only changes online scoring; the decoder remains a single static dictionary `B={b_i}`.

LUTurbo/Lottable export path: export the static dictionary vectors `b_i`; online inference computes coefficients/support, then runs standard lookup plus weighted sum. No dynamic vector generation is introduced.
Compatibility judgment: `直接兼容`. Final reconstruction is a finite weighted sum over one static vector library.
Selection-cost rationale: quick table marks `factorized_topk` feasible through `EF=8`, with `EF=8` estimated around `1.12x h*n`, giving safer policy margin than budget-edge alternatives.

Round 112 policy rejection on `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual EF=4` is a negative signal for using another near-threshold extended-compatible family immediately; this round shifts only the main architecture family to a more conservative cost-feasible candidate.
next_hypotheses: If `factorized_topk EF=8` is feasible but too weak in FVU, probe `topk EF=6` as the simplest direct-compatible baseline at the exact budget edge.
If `factorized_topk EF=8` is competitive, test `factorized_topk EF=6` for a cheaper Pareto point.
If direct-compatible families are clearly too weak, return to `lowrank_residual EF=4` as the next extensible compatible family with comfortable cost headroom.

This tests whether a cheaper direct-compatible selector becomes preferable once the search is forced into the low-cost regime.

Risk: historical `factorized_topk` quality was weak at high EF, so low-EF feasibility may still come with poor FVU; however the current blocker is absence of any budget-feasible compatible point, so direct-compatible cost headroom is the priority.
